title: Rich Peruvian Chicken Aji De Gallina
description: A bitter, molten, and indulgent Peruvian dish featuring chicken, naan bread, and aji amarillo peppers, with a rich and creamy walnut sauce. This recipe combines the flavors of Peru with the crunch of toasted naan bread and the spiciness of aji amarillo peppers.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Season the chicken breast with salt and pepper.
Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Sear the chicken breast until browned on both sides, then transfer to the oven and bake for 20 minutes.
In a blender or food processor, puree the chopped aji amarillo peppers, walnuts, and garlic.
In a large saucepan, combine the pureed pepper mixture, heavy cream, and parmesan cheese.
Bring the sauce to a simmer over medium heat, whisking constantly.
Reduce the heat to low and let the sauce simmer for 5 minutes.
Slice the cooked chicken breast and serve with the creamy walnut sauce and toasted naan bread.
